title: aster novae-scotiae
intro: Aster novae-scotiae, also known as New England aster, is a flowering plant native to eastern North America, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Aster novae-scotiae include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce pain and inflammation, and even exhibit antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, are often used to treat various health conditions, and can be made by steeping the dried flowers, leaves, or roots in hot water.
